title: Dev Supervisor Ledger
description: Durable per-round state ledger for the dev-supervisor. Describes the shape of ledger.template.json. INVARIANT (cross-field, enforced by the validator script, not by JSON Schema): a task whose status is "done" MUST have verified === true AND a non-empty evidence array. JSON Schema draft-07 cannot express this conditional across the status/verified/evidence triplet reliably for all validators, so the validator enforces it; this schema documents and structurally constrains the rest.

round_id.description: Stable identifier for this supervisor round, e.g. round-<slug>-<yyyymmdd>.

goal.description: One-line statement of the round goal (the reinterpreted latest user request).

updated_local.description: Local timestamp of the last ledger write, e.g. '2026-06-17 10:51:00 local'. Free-form local time string; not required to be RFC 3339.

tasks.description: The round task list (one level deep, no epics). Each task carries its own status and verification proof.

title.description: Short imperative description of the task.

status.description: Explicit task state enum. INVARIANT: status 'done' requires verified === true and a non-empty evidence array (enforced in the validator script).

verified.description: True only when the task outcome has been proven against its acceptance criteria. Must be true when status is 'done'.

evidence.description: Proof artifacts for completion (command output, test names, file paths, links). Must be non-empty when status is 'done'.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable conditions the task must satisfy to be marked done.

files.description: Exact file paths this task owns, used for safe parallel worker fan-out (no two tasks write the same path).

depends_on.description: Ids of tasks that must complete before this one.

notes.description: Optional free-form notes, blockers, or correction trail for this task.

decisions.description: Append-only record of decisions taken this round (rationale preserved for audit).

risks.description: Open risks and their mitigations for this round.

mitigation.description: How the risk is reduced or contained.
